meta.description:
The journey continues in the magical world of Middle-earth in The Two Towers: Trick-Taking Game, a narrative-driven co-operative card game that lets players take on the roles of heroes and villains in Tolkien's legendary work, The Two Towers. This standalone game follows 2025's The Fellowship of the Ring: Trick-Taking Game with chapters 19-36 of gameplay. The chapters can be played in any order and playing the earlier game isn't required, but ideally you play them in sequence.

In each chapter, each player takes a different character role and each character has a condition that must be met in order to pass the chapter and advance in the story. As you advance through the chapters, new characters, items, and challenges are introduced to the game. The One Ring is the game's only trump card, but initially rings can't be led until someone plays one off-suit.

In the two-player game, one hand of cards is dealt to a dummy player, with some cards being face up and others face down. This dummy is assigned a character, and one of the human players will play cards for it based on which cards are free to be played.

In the solitaire game, one player plays four hands of face-up cards, with each hand being assigned a character and only a few cards being available at a time. After you play a trick, deal each hand a new card.

—description from the publisher
meta.wiki:
Contents

Cards: 144
  • 60 main deck cards:
    • 8 Hills suit (1 - 8)
    • 8 Mountains suit (1 - 8)
    • 8 Shadows suit (1 - 8)
    • 8 Forests suit (1 - 8)
    • 1 White Tower card
    • 1 Black Tower card
    • 3 Orcs cards
    • 5 Rings suit (1 - 5)
    • 2 Weariness cards
    • 16 chapter-specific cards:
      • 8 Fangorn Forest suit (9 - 16)
      • 6 Orcs cards
      • 2 Rings suit (6 - 7)
  • 18 title cards
  • 38 character cards
  • 25 event cards
  • 2 step cards
  • 1 gift card
Tokens: 11
  • 4 star tokens
  • 1 White Tower token
  • 1 Black Tower token
  • 4 Burden tokens (2 - 5)
  • 1 Rings token

Rulebook: 1

List of Cards

Each card has a small box in the bottom-right corner with the card's number in it. The color of the box determines whether it is used in the game's randomized endless mode, the Road Goes Ever On.

Cross-Compatibility

The following character cards from The Fellowship of the Ring: Trick-Taking Game are compatible with this game's version of the Road Goes Ever On:
  • 1.03 - Bilbo (Peculiar)
  • 1.04 - Gandalf (Grey Wizard)
  • 2.09 - Gildor Inglorion (High Elf)
  • 2.12 - Fatty Bolger (Cautious)
  • 2.16 - Tom Bombadil (The Master)
  • 2.19 - Goldberry (River's Daughter)
  • 2.22 - Barliaman Butterbur (Forgetful)
  • 2.28 - Glorfindel (Elf-lord)
  • 2.29 - Bill the Pony (Sturdy)
  • 2.36 - Arwen (The Evenstar)
  • 2.37 - Glóin (Bilbo's Companion)
  • 2.41 - Boromir (Captain of Gondor)
  • 2.46 - Gwaihir (Lord of the Wind)
  • 2.47 - Shadowfax (Lord of Horses)
  • 2.48 - Radagast (Brown Wizard)
  • 3.17 - Gandalf, Servant of the Secret Fire (Flame of Anor)
  • 3.25 - Galadriel (Lady of Lothlórien)
  • 3.26 - Celeborn (Lord of Lothlórien)
